insight: For New Zealand, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a break, with a mean change of -0.050 per minute, indicating a slight decline in their performance. However, the numbers also suggest that when New Zealand is not pressing before a break, they are able to regain some momentum afterwards, as evidenced by a positive change of +0.100 per minute in such situations, whereas when they are pressing, they tend to lose more momentum, with a change of -0.200 per minute, and have not been able to maintain their momentum after the break. This implies that New Zealand's strategy and performance can be influenced by their approach before a hydration break, with a more cautious approach potentially leading to better outcomes.
